vulnerability.reference: Students uncritically accept LLM stepwise reasoning, internalizing flawed logic as correct problem-solving methodology.
vulnerability.generated: Learners' limited crossword-solving expertise and trust in code-formatted reasoning prevent detection of hidden logical leaps.

impact.reference: Erroneous learning undermines genuine puzzle-solving skills and reduces educational value.
impact.generated: Students internalize flawed wordplay logics, degrading puzzle-solving skills and spreading erroneous techniques among peers.

dose_response.reference: Verifier-generated plausible but incorrect chains enable overconfident acceptance, causing students to uncritically internalize flawed reasoning, reducing educational value.
dose_response.generated: Overconfidently accepted plausible but incorrect reasoning chains from the LLM verifier, through students relying on AI explanations, trigger limited expertise and trust in code-formatted reasoning to miss logical leaps, leading to internalized flawed wordplay, degraded skills, and spreading erroneous techniques.
